name: Noam Carmon
title: Data Scientist · ML Team Lead
summary: Data scientist with 5+ years owning the full ML lifecycle — from research and evaluation design to shipping models into production. Experienced in tabular/time‑series ML and messy data (noisy labels, ambiguity, long‑tail imbalance) to improve robustness and evaluation reliability. Independent applied research on a GRPO‑based chess model.
about: Focused on moving research into production, handling real‑world data mess, and shipping ML systems that work in practice. Most of my experience comes from ML projects on tabular and time‑series data.

highlights: Owned end‑to‑end ML delivery (research → evaluation → deployment) while leading a 6‑person team and remaining hands‑on in model development.
Improved recall and precision by refining noisy labels and addressing long‑tail imbalance with heuristics and balanced resampling.
Mitigated prediction flickering in near‑real time by adding temporal smoothing.
Built reproducible training + evaluation pipelines from raw data to ensembles, including domain feature engineering and sliding‑window time‑series features.
Developed and deployed multi‑class models on tabular/time‑series data with emphasis on long‑tail class imbalance.
Improved fine‑grained separability by designing specialist model ensembles for ambiguous, imbalanced datasets.

upcoming.title: llm-gatekeeping
upcoming.summary: Using an LLM‑as‑classifier to detect jailbreak attacks and gate unsafe prompts before they reach models.

projects.title: grpo-chess-model
projects.summary: Independent applied research on a GRPO‑based chess model. End‑to‑end deep RL pipeline with a transformer, GRPO on noisy rewards, data sampling, and reproducible evaluation tracking with multi‑level Stockfish benchmarks.
